The core contribution is an anomaly-aware, multivariate forecasting framework that not only predicts donation volume but specifically identifies upcoming donation drought periods

The project bridges the gap between traditional textile manufacturing and data-driven optimization by focusing on the relationship between yarn parameters and loom performance.

items.name: OpenPQD-Synthetic-Power-Quality-Dataset-Analysis-Toolkit-for-ML
items.description: An open-source dataset of synthetic power quality disturbances (PQD), ideal for training and validating machine learning models. Signals are generated using established mathematical models from scientific literature, with parameter ranges conforming to the IEEE 1159 standard. Includes a Python analysis framework for statistical exploration.

items.name: awesome-machine-learning
items.description: A curated list of awesome Machine Learning frameworks, libraries and software.

items.name: Predictive-Maintenance-and-Simulation-Framework-for-Aircraft-Fleet-Operations
items.description: This project develops a predictive maintenance framework for aircraft turbofan engines using NASA's C-MAPSS dataset. It combines machine learning (LightGBM) for Remaining Useful Life (RUL) prediction with discrete-event simulation (SimPy) to model the impact of maintenance queue delays on engine fleet health.

items.name: credit_risk_default_prediction

items.name: fair-post-processing
items.description: A modular Python framework for fairness-aware post-processing in machine learning.  The library takes classifier probabilities and learns optimal decision thresholds  by optimizing multiple fairness and performance metrics, enabling multi-objective  selection via methods such as TOPSIS and Zenith.

items.name: ML_Algorithms_From_Scratch

This repository serves as an educational resource to understand the inner workings of various ML models without relying on high-level frameworks like scikit-learn for the core logic.

items.name: ml-eof-framework-for-enso-prediction
items.description: A hybrid machine-learning and EOF-based framework for long-lead ENSO prediction using ocean–atmosphere predictors (Niño-3.4 SST, SOI, and warm water volume). items.description: This project builds a predictive analytics framework to forecast children in HHS care under the UAC Program. Using time-series and machine learning models, it converts historical data into forward-looking insights to support proactive planning and manage future care demand and system pressure.
